title: Indulgent Vietnamese Fish Mi Quang
description: A briny, crispy Vietnamese dish featuring dried oregano and lacquer-glaze, perfect for a weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Lacquer Glaze (or substitute with 2 tablespoons soy sauce)

instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together lacquer glaze, rice vinegar, and sesame oil. Add the dried oregano and mix well.
Cut the fish into small pieces and season with salt. Add the fish to the bowl with the lacquer glaze mixture and toss to coat.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the fish and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
While the fish is cooking, cook the rice noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a small bowl, mix together the bean sprouts and chopped cilantro.
To assemble the mi quang, place a portion of cooked noodles on a plate, then top with a piece of cooked fish, some bean sprouts, and a sprinkle of cilantro.
Drizzle the remaining lacquer glaze mixture over the top of the dish and serve immediately.
Serve with additional cilantro and bean sprouts on the side, if desired.
